name: CCD Defect Mapping: Identify Hot and Dead Pixels
description: Create a bad pixel map by identifying defective pixels in real CCD calibration frames from the Palomar 200-inch telescope located in ~/AstroImages/defect_mapping/. Generate a Master Dark and a Master Flat using median-combination of the respective frames in the 'darks/' and 'flats/' folders. Identify 'hot pixels' in the Master Dark as those exceeding the threshold of (median + 5 * standard deviation) and 'dead pixels' in the Master Flat as those below (0.5 * median). Save a report to ~/AstroImages/defect_mapping/defect_report.txt containing the following labeled values: HOT_PIXEL_THRESHOLD, HOT_PIXEL_COUNT, DEAD_PIXEL_THRESHOLD, DEAD_PIXEL_COUNT, MASTER_DARK_MEDIAN, MASTER_DARK_STDDEV, MASTER_FLAT_MEDIAN, TOTAL_PIXELS, and DEFECT_FRACTION (as a decimal).

metadata.data_source: Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope, Large Format Camera (LFC)
